back_translation: Elisha had said to the woman whose son he had restored to life, “Arise, you and your household, and go live as a foreigner wherever you can, for the LORD has decreed a seven-year famine on the land.”
So the woman did as the man of God said, and she and her household lived as foreigners in the land of the Philistines seven years.
At the end of seven years the woman returned from the land of the Philistines and went to the king to appeal for her house and her land.
Now the king was speaking to Gehazi, the servant of the man of God, saying, “Tell me all the great things Elisha has done.”

As Gehazi was telling the king how Elisha had raised the dead, behold, the very woman whose son Elisha had revived came to appeal to the king for her house and land. So Gehazi said, “My lord the king, this is the woman, and this is her son whom Elisha restored to life.”

When the king questioned the woman, she confirmed it. So the king appointed an official for her, saying, “Restore all that was hers, with all the produce of the field from the day she left the land until now.”
Elisha went to Damascus while Ben-hadad king of Aram was sick, and the king was told, “The man of God has come here.”
So the king said to Hazael, “Take a gift in your hand, go to meet the man of God, and inquire of the LORD through him, ‘Will I recover from this illness?’”

So Hazael went to meet Elisha, taking with him a gift of all the good things of Damascus, forty camel loads; he went in and stood before him and said, “Your son Ben-hadad king of Aram has sent me to ask, ‘Will I recover from this illness?’”
Elisha said, “Go and tell him, ‘You will surely recover,’ but the LORD has shown me that in fact he will die.”
Elisha fixed his gaze on him until he was embarrassed, and then the man of God wept.

Hazael asked, “Why does my lord weep?” Elisha answered, “Because I know the evil you will do to the Israelites: you will burn their fortresses, kill their young men with the sword, dash their little ones to pieces, and rip open their pregnant women.”
Hazael said, “Your servant is but a dog; how could he do such a great thing?” Elisha answered, “The LORD has shown me that you will be king over Aram.”
So Hazael left Elisha and returned to his master, who asked him, “What did Elisha say to you?” He answered, “He told me you would surely recover.”
But the next day Hazael took a thick cloth, dipped it in water, and spread it over the king’s face, so that he died; and Hazael reigned in his place.
In the fifth year of Joram son of Ahab king of Israel, Jehoram son of Jehoshaphat became king of Judah.
Jehoram was thirty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.
He walked in the way of the kings of Israel, as the house of Ahab had done, for he married a daughter of Ahab; and he did evil in the sight of the LORD.
Yet for the sake of His servant David the LORD was unwilling to destroy Judah, since He had promised to give a lamp to David and his descendants forever.
In his days Edom rebelled against the hand of Judah and set up their own king.
So Jehoram crossed over to Zair with all his chariots; and when the Edomites surrounded him and his chariot commanders, he rose by night and attacked, but his troops fled to their homes.
So Edom has been in rebellion against the hand of Judah to this day; and Libnah rebelled at the same time.
As for the rest of the acts of Jehoram and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
And Jehoram rested with his fathers and was buried with them in the City of David; and Ahaziah his son reigned in his place.
In the twelfth year of Joram son of Ahab king of Israel, Ahaziah son of Jehoram became king of Judah.
Ahaziah was twenty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned one year in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Athaliah, granddaughter of Omri king of Israel.
He walked in the way of the house of Ahab and did evil in the sight of the LORD like the house of Ahab, for he was a son-in-law of the house of Ahab.
Ahaziah went with Joram son of Ahab to fight against Hazael king of Aram at Ramoth-gilead, and the Arameans wounded Joram.

So Joram returned to Jezreel to be healed of the wounds the Arameans had given him at Ramah when he fought against Hazael king of Aram. And Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah went down to Jezreel to visit Joram son of Ahab, because he was wounded.
